title: 42 Questions to Ask a Buyer's Agent
content:
Comprehensive guide for home buyers evaluating potential real estate agents.

GENERAL QUESTIONS (1-10):
- Ask about tenure in real estate; caution with less than 2 years experience
- Full-time agents preferred over part-time
- Ask about dual agency practices (can streamline but creates conflicts of interest)
- What makes the agent stand out? Do they work with first-time buyers?

EXPERIENCE & EXPERTISE (12-18):
- Agent should demonstrate knowledge of specific property types and neighborhoods
- Average agent completes 12 transaction sides per year (benchmark)
- Professional certifications indicate commitment to ongoing education

SHOWINGS (19-22):
- Ask about access to off-market listings for competitive advantages in seller's markets

COMMUNICATION (23-27):
- Establish preferred contact methods and update frequency
- Weekend and evening availability for working buyers

NEGOTIATION & OFFERS (28-34):
- Ask about escalation clauses, modified contingencies, earnest money strategies
- Negotiations cover closing date, financing method, contingencies beyond just price

FEES & CONTRACTS (35-38):
- Understand commission structures and additional costs before signing buyer agency agreements

ADDITIONAL SERVICES (39-42):
- Agent should recommend trusted mortgage brokers, home inspectors, title companies
